So I would Like you to read this following interview I drafted of Selim Barkat, Country manager of HBL and turn the whole interview into a well-written op-ed of Selim with the same content and add more relevant stuff if necessary. - "‘HBL’s international network helps support businesses in Bangladesh’
INTERVIEW
Habib Bank Limited (HBL) – an international private commercial bank, majority owned by The Aga Khan Fund for Economic Development (AKFED) and headquartered in Pakistan – has been operating in Bangladesh for over four decades. Selim Barkat, Bangladesh country manager of HBL, recently spoke to The Business Standard’s Staff Correspondent Tonmoy Modak. In this interview, Selim Barkat discusses a wide range of subjects, including the legacy of HBL in Bangladesh, how the bank has evolved to meet the changing needs of its customers and the banking industry, what sets HBL’s offerings apart from other banks in the region, and how HBL collaborates with local businesses and industries to support their growth and enable them to expand globally.
 
Can you discuss the legacy of HBL in Bangladesh, and how it has evolved to meet the changing needs of customers and the banking industry?
Certainly, HBL has a long-standing legacy in Bangladesh, dating back over four decades when it first established its branch in Bangladesh in 1976. Over the years, we have played a meaningful role in supporting the growth and development of the country’s economy, providing a range of financial services to individuals and businesses alike.
As the banking industry has evolved, we’ve adapted to meet our customers’ changing needs by offering innovative solutions and a wide range of products and services. We’ve remained nimble and flexible in meeting the evolving needs of our customers, from traditional banking services like deposits and loans to trade and payment solutions.
I must make a note of the ownership change whereby the Aga Khan Fund for Economic Development (AKFED) – the for-profit arm of the Aga Khan Development Network (AKDN) obtained the majority stake in the bank and changed the course of its progress.
With a vision to transform the bank into one of the best financial institutions in whichever market the bank operates in, we have adjusted our strategy and focus to creating value propositions for our clients and the markets. It truly means putting the client first in everything we do and be the most compliant bank.
In recent years, we have been investing in our technological capabilities with a focus on becoming a “technology company with a banking licence.” This investment will enable us to offer digital banking services like online banking, mobile banking, and other value-added services to enhance our customer experience. At the heart of our initiatives are our clients and the country we serve.
 
What makes HBL’s offerings unique in comparison to other banks in the country and the region?
We prioritise our customers and aim to provide them with trustworthy and customised financial solutions. What sets us apart from other banks in the region is our presence in Bangladesh, as well as our international network across 14 countries of significant financial importance. We offer a wide range of services, including corporate and investment banking, commercial banking, and retail banking. For our top-tier local corporate customers, as they grow in size and embark on the ambition to become global players, we stand beside them to help them achieve that aspiration. As we stand today many of our clients are dependent on supply chains linking to these countries. This gives us a unique opportunity to facilitate our clients’ businesses with end-to-end services on these cross-border transactions and help the clients to continue to grow.

How does HBL collaborate with local businesses and industries in Bangladesh to support their growth and enable them to expand globally?
We work closely with local businesses and industries in Bangladesh to offer a range of financial products and services tailored to their unique needs, including trade finance, working capital finance, and term finance. Additionally, we provide specialised expertise and advice to help businesses navigate the complex regulatory and business environment in Bangladesh, which can facilitate their growth and expansion.  We also collaborate with industry associations and business groups to promote entrepreneurship and innovation in Bangladesh.
Furthermore, as mentioned earlier, our international network enables local businesses to expand globally, providing them with access to new markets and investment opportunities. Through these efforts, we are committed to promoting economic growth and prosperity in Bangladesh while supporting local businesses in their journey toward global expansion.
 
Could you elaborate on the significance of the trade relationship between China and Bangladesh, and how HBL is contributing to facilitating it?
China is Bangladesh’s largest trading partner, and the two countries have been actively working to expand their trade ties in recent years. As an international bank with a strong presence in both China and Bangladesh, we play an important role in facilitating trade and other economic activities between the two countries.
We have established a dedicated China Desk, which is staffed by Chinese and Bangladeshi professionals and directly services our clients who are engaged in the China-Bangladesh trade. We are also facilitating large Chinese companies who are engaged in our government’s mega infrastructure projects. Our role is often facilitating these companies with their local financing needs as well as advising them of the regulatory parameters that they must abide by. Our ability to advise them of the local banking regulations has substantially contributed to the ease of doing business for these companies. We also provide RMB-denominated transactions for corporates and financial institutions and have been at the forefront of providing RMB remittance services for Chinese nationals working in Bangladesh. Our partnership with the Chinese banking sector has enabled us to develop the necessary expertise and infrastructure to support this expanding demand.

How does HBL’s international network help it to support businesses in Bangladesh?
Our global network is crucial in supporting businesses in Bangladesh, providing international banking services such as trade finance, foreign currency accounts, and fund transfers. Our presence in multiple countries also offers local businesses access to wider markets and investment opportunities. For instance, through our branches in China, we assist Bangladeshi businesses in establishing partnerships and transactions with Chinese companies. Our expertise and experience can also offer valuable guidance to businesses seeking global expansion. We also facilitate international companies operating in Bangladesh.

How has HBL’s association with AKFED influenced its approach to fostering economic growth and development in Bangladesh and other areas?
As the flagship company of the Aga Khan Fund for Economic Development, HBL shares AKDN’s commitment to promoting economic development and sustainability. Our association with AKFED shapes our business approach and prioritises adherence to local laws and regulations and good governance. The values of excellence, integrity, progressiveness, customer focus, and meritocracy, are instilled by the Board in our organisation, reflecting our affiliation with AKFED. This partnership enables us to take a more comprehensive and sustainable approach toward supporting economic growth and development in Bangladesh and beyond.

How does HBL plan to continue its growth and expansion in Bangladesh in the coming years?
HBL is committed to expanding its business in Bangladesh with a strategic approach to benefit both our clients and the country’s prosperity. We have redefined our business strategy in Bangladesh, solely focused on adding value and staying relevant.
Our plan is to continue investing in our operations and infrastructure, and strengthen our relationships with local businesses and industries. We aim to explore new opportunities to support the country’s economy, including partnerships with government entities and development organisations. Furthermore, we intend to leverage our presence in markets with a large Bangladeshi population, mainly in the Middle East, to facilitate homebound remittances as part of our growth strategy."

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Title: Selim Barkat: Steering HBL towards Transformative Growth in Bangladesh and Beyond

As the country manager of Habib Bank Limited (HBL) in Bangladesh, Selim Barkat, has a unique perspective on the role of the international financial institution in the South Asian nation's growth and development. Operating in Bangladesh for over four decades, HBL has long been a key player in the burgeoning economy, supporting local businesses and fostering global expansion.

Under Barkat's leadership, HBL has adapted its strategy and offerings to meet the ever-changing needs of the banking industry, emphasizing innovation and flexibility to better serve the country's business community.

Lasting Legacy and Evolution

Since its establishment in Bangladesh in 1976, HBL has played a significant role in the nation's economic growth, providing a variety of financial services for the local market. This long-standing legacy was further solidified by the entry of the Aga Khan Fund for Economic Development (AKFED), which acquired a majority stake in the bank and charted a new course for the financial institution.

With the renewed leadership, Barkat has steered HBL towards transformative growth, prioritizing value creation for clients and the market at large. Reflecting the bank's new motto of "technology company with a banking licence", HBL has recently invested substantially in technological capabilities, enabling the delivery of digital banking services that enhance overall customer experience.

HBL: A Unique and Customer-Centric Approach

At the cornerstone of HBL's evolving strategy lies a dedication to prioritizing customers and ensuring their individual needs are met. The bank's unique offering lies in its presence not only in Bangladesh but also its international network across 14 financially significant countries on the global stage. As such, local clients looking to expand globally can rely on HBL's robust support and end-to-end services in cross-border transactions.

Through this interconnected network, Barkat emphasizes the bank's commitment to working closely with businesses and industries across the nation. HBL's tailored approach through trade finance, working capital finance, and term finance ensures that customers can take advantage of new markets and investment opportunities afforded by the bank's global reach.

Catalyst for Growth

HBL's position as a bridge between Bangladesh and other countries is crucial for the support of local businesses. Central to this is the bank's facilitation of trade with China – the nation's largest trading partner. HBL's China Desk, a dedicated group providing consultation and services for clients engaged in China-Bangladesh trade, serves as a prime example of the bank's impact on expanding the trade relationship.

HBL's international network continues to provide essential services, such as remittance support for Bangladeshi migrant workers in the Middle East, where the bank has a strong presence. With an eye on global expansion and partnerships with government entities and development organizations, Barkat envisions HBL serving a pivotal role in Bangladesh's economic growth and international connectivity.

Shared Values and Financial Inclusion

HBL's partnership with AKFED also shapes the bank's approach to fostering sustainable economic growth and development. Grounded in values like excellence, integrity, progressiveness, customer focus, and meritocracy, the affiliation with AKFED ensures that HBL promotes not only financial services but also inclusion and responsible business practices.

As Barkat looks to the future and HBL's continuing growth in Bangladesh, one thing is certain: the bank's customer-centric approach, global vision, and commitment to sustainable development will ensure that HBL remains a crucial partner for local businesses navigating the complex world of finance and globalization.

Title: Embracing Innovation and Global Connectivity: HBL's Vision for Bangladesh's Economic Prosperity

As the Country Manager of Habib Bank Limited (HBL) in Bangladesh, I am fortunate to lead one of the most innovative, forward-thinking banking institutions in the region. With a legacy dating back over four decades in Bangladesh, our bank has continuously evolved in line with the changing needs of customers and the banking sector as a whole.

A Changing Landscape: Adapting and Innovating

Under my leadership, the bank has undergone a paradigm shift, which started with the entry of the Aga Khan Fund for Economic Development (AKFED) as a majority stakeholder. This partnership has cemented our commitment to operating with integrity, customer focus, and value creation.

Today, guided by the vision of transforming HBL into a "technology company with a banking license," we are investing in technology and digital banking services to enhance the customer experience. By staying true to our core values of excellence, progressiveness, and meritocracy, we are helping both local and international businesses thrive. 

Standing Apart: Leveraging Our Global Network

At HBL, our focus is on delivering customized banking solutions that are tailored to our customers' needs. We recognize that the success of Bangladesh's business community often depends on their ability to expand globally. As a bank with an international network across 14 key financial markets, we are uniquely positioned to help them achieve this goal.

Whether through trade finance, working capital finance or term finance, we strive to provide our customers with products and services that enable them to grow and prosper in the global marketplace.

A Bridge Between Bangladesh and China

One example that highlights our global impact on Bangladesh's economy is the strong trade relationship between Bangladesh and China. As an international bank with a presence in both countries, we play a crucial role in facilitating economic activities and trade between the two nations. Our dedicated China Desk, comprised of Chinese and Bangladeshi professionals, provides consultation and services to clients engaged in China-Bangladesh trade, ensuring that both nations continue to prosper.

A Bright Future with HBL

Looking ahead to the future of HBL in Bangladesh, our growth strategy centers on a continued investment in technology and fostering strong relationships with local businesses. In addition, our international presence helps facilitate remittances for Bangladeshi migrant workers, particularly in the Middle East, providing a critical lifeline to families back home.

In our association with AKFED, we strive for not only growth but sustainable development that can enrich the lives of people in Bangladesh and beyond. We are proud to support local businesses and contribute to the country's prosperity as they embark on their journey of global expansion.

By embracing innovation, prioritizing our customers, and connecting Bangladesh to global markets, I believe HBL is poised to support Bangladesh's economic growth in the years to come. Together with our clients and partners, we can build an even brighter future for the nation.

Title: The Path to Economic Prosperity in Bangladesh: A Reflection on the Role of Innovation and Global Connectivity

As the Country Manager of a prominent international bank in Bangladesh, it has been my privilege to witness firsthand the enormous growth and development in the nation over the past few decades. Alongside my colleagues, we strive to make a positive impact on the lives of our customers by embracing innovation and providing personalized banking solutions.

A Changing Landscape: Adaptation and Innovation

The world of banking has evolved rapidly in recent years, largely driven by technological advancements and digitization. To ensure we remain valuable to our customers, our goal has been to adapt and innovate while embracing the vision of becoming a "technology company with a banking license." I am proud of the progress we have made in this area and the positive impact these changes have had on our clients and the broader economy.

Connecting Businesses to the World

My dedication to our customers extends beyond the borders of Bangladesh. A vital aspect of our continued success has been the ability to connect our clients to global markets, enabling them to expand their operations and thrive on the international stage. Our presence in more than a dozen key financial markets worldwide facilitates cross-border transactions that support businesses and strengthen economic ties between countries.

Uniting Bangladesh and China

One particularly notable example of our global connectivity has been our role in strengthening the trade relationship between Bangladesh and China. Given the importance of this trading partnership, we have established a dedicated team of professionals to support clients engaged in trade and investment between these two nations. This team plays an essential part in fostering the bilateral commercial connections that positively impact both countries.

Looking Ahead: A Humble Vision for the Future

As we continue to provide services to our clients in Bangladesh and beyond, my vision for the future is focused on growth, collaboration, and sustainability. I aim to continue fostering strong relationships with local businesses and industry associations, while exploring new opportunities for us to contribute meaningfully to the country's prosperity.

One area of ongoing importance is our work in facilitating remittances from Bangladeshi workers living overseas, particularly in the Middle East, as it significantly impacts the lives of many families in the country.

In conclusion, I believe that by adapting and embracing innovative technologies, prioritizing the needs of our clients, and maintaining a humble, customer-centric approach, we can continue to empower businesses and individuals in Bangladesh on their journey toward financial success. Ultimately, through strong global connections and mutual collaboration, we can build a brighter future for our nation.

Dhaka, Bangladesh, 19 November, 2022: Muhammad Aurangzeb, President & CEO – HBL, a seasoned banker with over 30 years of experience, visits Dhaka between 22-23 November 2022. In this visit he will engage with government, regulatory bodies, clients and partners to deliberate upon technology driven, client centric banking solutions and foster foreign direct investment in the country. Faisal Lalani, Head of International Banking - HBL, will accompany him.
 
HBL, a bank of regional relevance with operations spread across multiple geographies, has been serving clients in Bangladesh for more than 45 years. 

Muhammad Aurangzeb joined HBL on 30 April 2018 as the President & CEO. Prior to this responsibility at HBL, Mr. Aurangzeb was the CEO for JP Morgan’s Global Corporate Bank based in Asia, with a rich international banking experience of over 30 years in other senior management roles at ABN AMRO and RBS based in Amsterdam and Singapore. He received his BS and MBA degrees from the Wharton School (University of Pennsylvania).

During his meetings with key stakeholders in Bangladesh, Muhammad Aurangzeb will draw attention to the fact that HBL Bangladesh offers RMB/CNY (Renminbi) denominated account to its respected clients and how HBL's presence in China is enabling the bank to conduct business with state-owned firms and prominent financial institutions in China. HBL will continue to play its part in the transformation of the banking system in Bangladesh, consistent with its vision of becoming a technology company with a banking license.
About HBL
HBL, operating in Bangladesh for more than 45 years, is a bank of regional relevance with operations spread across multiple geographies. It is headquartered out of Pakistan where it has the largest operations.  The Bank is a leading full-service commercial bank. HBL is recognized as the leading financial institution in this region for its client-centric innovation in financial services. The Aga Khan Fund for Economic Development (AKFED) owns 51% shareholding and the management control of HBL while the remaining shareholding is held by individuals, local and foreign institutions and funds including CDC Group Plc and International Finance Corporation. Muhammad Aurangzeb's Extensive Banker's Profile

1) FARHAN TALIB
REGIONAL GENERAL MANAGER – SINGAPORE & CHINA
HABIB BANK LTD

Mr. Farhan Talib is the Regional General Manager for Singapore and China and currently based in Singapore since February 2020. He has been with HBL since January 2010. In December 2013 he was the Chief Representative based in Beijing, China. He was instrumental in setting up HBL’s first branch in Urumqi, China and establishing HBL’s footprint in China. In July 2017 he moved back to Pakistan as the Head of Corporate and Investment Banking and continue to oversee HBL’s China business. Farhan works closely with HBL team in Bangladesh to support corporates in their business initiatives in China , Singapore and other international markets. 

2) Cheng Wei (Amanda)
Country Manager, China , Branch Manager, HBL Beijing and Head of FIs - China

Amanda joined HBL Beijing Representative Office in 2005 since its establishment. Since then, she has been instrumental to formulate the Bank’s strategy as well as implementation of the same in China.  In line with the overall strategy to “Look East”, the Bank has extensively leveraged opportunities arised from Belt and Road Initiative and the RMB internationalization.  These have enabled HBL to be considered in China market as the positive example and emerging financial institutions from the countries alongside Belt and Road.
In 2016, Amanda participated in the preparatory team for establishment of the Urumqi Branch, the Bank’s first branch in China.  In September of the same year, she was transferred to Urumqi Branch taking position as General Manager – Business Development Department.   In July 2017, upon approval from regulators she was transferred back to Beijing and appointed as the Chief Representative of Beijing Representative Office.  Since then, she started lead the efforts to ensure obtaining of the regulatory approval for establishment of Beijing Branch in 2020, and together with team ensured the successful commercial launch of Beijing Branch. This has set an important milestone to mark HBL as the first bank from South Asia, Middle East, and East Africa to establish a branch in Beijing with onshore RMB service capabilities. 
Before joining HBL, she worked with ICBC for 3 years.   Amanda holds Master's degree in Cross Cultural Communication and International Management from the University of Newcastle upon Tyne, UK.

turns.text:
Title: Two Top HBL Regional Executives Visit Dhaka to Boost Business Relations and Capitalize on Opportunities

Dhaka, Bangladesh, [Enter Date]: Farhan Talib, Regional General Manager – Singapore & China for Habib Bank Limited (HBL), and Cheng Wei (Amanda), Country Manager (China), Branch Manager of HBL Beijing, and Head of Financial Institutions (FIs) - China, are set to visit Dhaka shortly. The visit aims to explore new opportunities to bolster business ties between Bangladesh, China, and Singapore.

Farhan Talib: Building HBL's Presence in Singapore and China

Mr. Farhan Talib has been with HBL since January 2010, holding several key positions. In December 2013, he was appointed Chief Representative in Beijing, China, where he played a pivotal role in establishing HBL's first branch in Urumqi, China. In July 2017, he moved back to Pakistan to serve as the Head of Corporate and Investment Banking, while continuing to oversee HBL's China business. Now based in Singapore since February 2020, Mr. Talib works closely with the HBL team in Bangladesh to support local corporates in their business endeavors across China, Singapore, and other international markets.

Cheng Wei (Amanda): Pioneering HBL's Expansion in China

A vital member of HBL Beijing Representative Office since its inception in 2005, Amanda has been actively formulating and implementing the bank's strategy in the Chinese market. In line with HBL's "Look East" strategy, Amanda has successfully leveraged opportunities arising from the Belt and Road Initiative and Renminbi (RMB) internationalization. Her efforts have positioned HBL as an emerging financial institution from the Belt and Road region. 

In 2016, Amanda was part of the preparatory team for the establishment of the Urumqi branch, and later served as the General Manager of Business Development at the branch. As of July 2017, she returned to Beijing, leading the efforts to secure regulatory approval for the Beijing Branch in 2020. Amanda's tireless work contributed to the successful commercial launch of the branch, setting an important milestone for HBL as the first bank from South Asia, Middle East, and East Africa to establish a branch in Beijing with onshore RMB services.

During their visit to Dhaka, the two top HBL executives will meet with key stakeholders, clients, and partners to discuss technology-driven banking solutions and foreign direct investment in the country. These meetings aim to further strengthen and expand HBL's presence in Bangladesh, China, and Singapore while continuing to support the growth of local businesses in these regions.

About HBL

HBL is a leading full-service commercial bank with over 45 years of presence in Bangladesh. With headquarters in Pakistan and operations spread across multiple geographies, it is recognized as a significant regional player. The Aga Khan Fund for Economic Development (AKFED) owns 51% of HBL's shares, with other local and international shareholders, including CDC Group Plc and International Finance Corporation. Under their strong leadership, HBL has been the driving force for client-centric innovation in financial services in the region.
